data_in.description: Valid mzPeak file (binary format compliant with draft mzPeak specification)
Python mzPeak reader implementation from mobiusklein/mzpeak_prototyping repository

data_out.description: Structured tabular artifact (Parquet or CSV file) containing parsed spectrum data with annotated columns (m/z values, intensities, spectrum metadata)

domain_knowledge: The mzPeak format is a draft specification designed for scalable and interoperable mass spectrometry data storage, as documented in the living specification at https://hupo-psi.github.io/mzPeak-specification/.
The Python implementation in mobiusklein/mzpeak_prototyping provides read-only access to mzPeak files and does not currently support writing functionality.
PyArrow is used as the underlying serialization and tabular data structure library for the Python mzPeak reader implementation.
The project is in work-in-progress status with no stability guarantee, so API and format may change.
Spectrum data loaded from mzPeak files should include mass-to-charge (m/z) values, intensity measurements, and associated metadata fields per the draft specification.

direct_checks: script_runs: execute Python loader script from python/ subdirectory without errors
file_format_is: output artifact is valid Parquet file or pandas DataFrame serialized to standard format (pickle, CSV, or Parquet)
field_present: output table contains at least one column representing m/z values
field_present: output table contains at least one column representing intensity values
row_count_equals: output table has row_count > 0 (non-empty spectrum data)
output_matches_reference: if reference mzPeak file and expected output are available in repository, verify loaded DataFrame schema and row count match reference specification
expert_review: Verify that loaded spectrum data types (m/z and intensity) are appropriate for mass spectrometry (numeric, not truncated or corrupted)
Confirm that tabular structure correctly represents the logical hierarchy of mzPeak format (e.g., spectra, scans, peaks are correctly denested or indexed)
Assess whether pyarrow library integration follows Arrow memory model semantics correctly (zero-copy semantics, column-store layout)

cmd: import pyarrow.parquet as pq; import sys; sys.path.insert(0, '{inputs.python_reader}'); from mzpeak_reader import load_mzpeak; data = load_mzpeak('{inputs.mzpeak_file}'); table = data.to_arrow() if hasattr(data, 'to_arrow') else data; pq.write_table(table, 'output.parquet')

methodology_summary: Access the Python mzPeak reader implementation from the mobiusklein/mzpeak_prototyping repository.
Load the mzPeak binary file using the Python reader module with pyarrow as the backing table library.
Convert the in-memory spectrum data structure to a pandas DataFrame or pyarrow Table.
Serialize the tabular data to Parquet format for structured output and downstream interoperability.
Validation: verify that the output Parquet file contains all spectrum records with correctly parsed m/z and intensity columns and matches the row count of the source mzPeak file.

workflow_description: 1. Clone or access the mobiusklein/mzpeak_prototyping repository and navigate to the python/ subdirectory. 2. Import the Python mzPeak reader module and pyarrow library. 3. Load the mzPeak file using the Python read implementation. 4. Convert the loaded spectrum data into a pandas DataFrame or pyarrow Table structure. 5. Export the structured table to a Parquet file or CSV format for downstream use.
